title: Pan Seared Japanese Pork Dumplings
description: Buttery and flaky Japanese pork dumplings, a crowd-pleaser from the Tohoku region, featuring a hint of cayenne pepper, made without onion or garlic, and steamed to perfection in a bamboo steamer.

ingredients.name: ground pork
ingredients.notes: preferably from the loin or shoulder

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper, and mix until just combined.
Add the cold unsalted butter to the pork mixture, and use your fingers or a pastry blender to work the butter into the pork until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the flour to the pork mixture, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Gradually add the water to the dough, and mix until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface, and knead for 5-10 minutes, until the dough becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 20-25 equal pieces, and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk, and place a small spoonful of water in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the water, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Place the dumplings in a bamboo steamer lined with parchment paper, leaving some space between each dumpling to allow for expansion.
Steam the dumplings over boiling water for 10-15 minutes, until cooked through.
Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, and pan-fry the steamed dumplings until golden brown and crispy on the bottom.
Serve the pan-seared dumplings hot, garnished with chopped scallions and a dipping sauce of your choice.
